NKJV.1: O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongs— O God, to whom vengeance belongs, shine forth!
NKJV.2: Rise up, O Judge of the earth; Render punishment to the proud.
NKJV.3: LORD, how long will the wicked, How long will the wicked triumph?
NKJV.4: They utter speech, and speak insolent things; All the workers of iniquity boast in themselves.
NKJV.5: They break in pieces Your people, O LORD, And afflict Your heritage.
NKJV.6: They slay the widow and the stranger, And murder the fatherless.
NKJV.7: Yet they say, “The LORD does not see, Nor does the God of Jacob understand.”
NKJV.8: Understand, you senseless among the people; And you fools, when will you be wise?
NKJV.9: He who planted the ear, shall He not hear? He who formed the eye, shall He not see?
NKJV.10: He who instructs the nations, shall He not correct, He who teaches man knowledge?
NKJV.11: The LORD knows the thoughts of man, That they are futile.
NKJV.12: Blessed is the man whom You instruct, O LORD, And teach out of Your law,
NKJV.13: That You may give him rest from the days of adversity, Until the pit is dug for the wicked.
NKJV.14: For the LORD will not cast off His people, Nor will He forsake His inheritance.
NKJV.15: But judgment will return to righteousness, And all the upright in heart will follow it.
NKJV.16: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? Who will stand up for me against the workers of iniquity?
NKJV.17: Unless the LORD had been my help, My soul would soon have settled in silence.
NKJV.18: If I say, “My foot slips,” Your mercy, O LORD, will hold me up.
NKJV.19: In the multitude of my anxieties within me, Your comforts delight my soul.
NKJV.20: Shall the throne of iniquity, which devises evil by law, Have fellowship with You?
NKJV.21: They gather together against the life of the righteous, And condemn innocent blood.
NKJV.22: But the LORD has been my defense, And my God the rock of my refuge.
NKJV.23: He has brought on them their own iniquity, And shall cut them off in their own wickedness; The LORD our God shall cut them off.

ESV.1: O LORD, God of vengeance, O God of vengeance, shine forth!
ESV.2: Rise up, O judge of the earth; repay to the proud what they deserve!
ESV.3: O LORD, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked exult?
ESV.4: They pour out their arrogant words; all the evildoers boast.
ESV.5: They crush your people, O LORD, and afflict your heritage.
ESV.6: They kill the widow and the sojourner, and murder the fatherless;
ESV.7: and they say, “The LORD does not see; the God of Jacob does not perceive.”
ESV.8: Understand, O dullest of the people! Fools, when will you be wise?
ESV.9: He who planted the ear, does he not hear? He who formed the eye, does he not see?
ESV.10: He who disciplines the nations, does he not rebuke? He who teaches man knowledge —
ESV.11: the LORD — knows the thoughts of man, that they are but a breath.
ESV.12: Blessed is the man whom you discipline, O LORD, and whom you teach out of your law,
ESV.13: to give him rest from days of trouble, until a pit is dug for the wicked.
ESV.14: For the LORD will not forsake his people; he will not abandon his heritage;
ESV.15: for justice will return to the righteous, and all the upright in heart will follow it.
ESV.16: Who rises up for me against the wicked? Who stands up for me against evildoers?
ESV.17: If the LORD had not been my help, my soul would soon have lived in the land of silence.
ESV.18: When I thought, “My foot slips,” your steadfast love, O LORD, held me up.
ESV.19: When the cares of my heart are many, your consolations cheer my soul.
ESV.20: Can wicked rulers be allied with you, those who frame  injustice by statute?
ESV.21: They band together against the life of the righteous and condemn the innocent to death.
ESV.22: But the LORD has become my stronghold, and my God the rock of my refuge.
ESV.23: He will bring back on them their iniquity and wipe them out for their wickedness; the LORD our God will wipe them out.

NASB.1: O LORD, God of vengeance, God of vengeance, shine forth!
NASB.2: Rise up, O Judge of the earth, Render recompense to the proud.
NASB.3: How long shall the wicked, O LORD, How long shall the wicked exult?
NASB.4: They pour forth [words], they speak arrogantly; All who do wickedness vaunt themselves.
NASB.5: They crush Your people, O LORD, And afflict Your heritage.
NASB.6: They slay the widow and the stranger And murder the orphans.
NASB.7: They have said, 'The LORD does not see, Nor does the God of Jacob pay heed.'
NASB.8: Pay heed, you senseless among the people; And when will you understand, stupid ones?
NASB.9: He who planted the ear, does He not hear? He who formed the eye, does He not see?
NASB.10: He who chastens the nations, will He not rebuke, [Even] He who teaches man knowledge?
NASB.11: The LORD knows the thoughts of man, That they are a [mere] breath.
NASB.12: Blessed is the man whom You chasten, O LORD, And whom You teach out of Your law;
NASB.13: That You may grant him relief from the days of adversity, Until a pit is dug for the wicked.
NASB.14: For the LORD will not abandon His people, Nor will He forsake His inheritance.
NASB.15: For judgment will again be righteous, And all the upright in heart will follow it.
NASB.16: Who will stand up for me against evildoers? Who will take his stand for me against those who do wickedness?
NASB.17: If the LORD had not been my help, My soul would soon have dwelt in [the abode of] silence.
NASB.18: If I should say, 'My foot has slipped,' Your lovingkindness, O LORD, will hold me up.
NASB.19: When my anxious thoughts multiply within me, Your consolations delight my soul.
NASB.20: Can a throne of destruction be allied with You, One which devises mischief by decree?
NASB.21: They band themselves together against the life of the righteous And condemn the innocent to death.
NASB.22: But the LORD has been my stronghold, And my God the rock of my refuge.
NASB.23: He has brought back their wickedness upon them And will destroy them in their evil; The LORD our God will destroy them.

NLT.1:
O LORD, the God of vengeance,
O God of vengeance, let your glorious justice shine forth!
NLT.2:
Arise, O Judge of the earth.
Give the proud what they deserve.
NLT.3:
How long, O LORD?
How long will the wicked be allowed to gloat?
NLT.4:
How long will they speak with arrogance?
How long will these evil people boast?
NLT.5:
They crush your people, LORD,
hurting those you claim as your own.
NLT.6:
They kill widows and foreigners
and murder orphans.
NLT.7:
“The LORD isn’t looking,” they say,
“and besides, the God of Israel doesn’t care.”
NLT.8:
Think again, you fools!
When will you finally catch on?
NLT.9:
Is he deaf — the one who made your ears?
Is he blind — the one who formed your eyes?
NLT.10:
He punishes the nations — won’t he also punish you?
He knows everything — doesn’t he also know what you are doing?
NLT.11:
The LORD knows people’s thoughts;
he knows they are worthless!
NLT.12:
Joyful are those you discipline, LORD,
those you teach with your instructions.
NLT.13:
You give them relief from troubled times
until a pit is dug to capture the wicked.
NLT.14:
The LORD will not reject his people;
he will not abandon his special possession.
NLT.15:
Judgment will again be founded on justice,
and those with virtuous hearts will pursue it.
NLT.16:
Who will protect me from the wicked?
Who will stand up for me against evildoers?
NLT.17:
Unless the LORD had helped me,
I would soon have settled in the silence of the grave.
NLT.18:
I cried out, “I am slipping!”
but your unfailing love, O LORD, supported me.
NLT.19:
When doubts filled my mind,
your comfort gave me renewed hope and cheer.
NLT.20:
Can unjust leaders claim that God is on their side —
leaders whose decrees permit injustice?
NLT.21:
They gang up against the righteous
and condemn the innocent to death.
NLT.22:
But the LORD is my fortress;
my God is the mighty rock where I hide.
NLT.23:
God will turn the sins of evil people back on them.
He will destroy them for their sins.
The LORD our God will destroy them.

WEB.1: Yahweh, you God to whom vengeance belongs, you God to whom vengeance belongs, shine out.
WEB.2: Rise up, you judge of the earth. Pay back the proud what they deserve.
WEB.3: Yahweh, how long will the wicked, how long will the wicked triumph?
WEB.4: They pour out arrogant words. All the evildoers boast.
WEB.5: They break your people in pieces, Yahweh, and afflict your heritage.
WEB.6: They kill the widow and the alien, and murder the fatherless.
WEB.7: They say, “Yah will not see, neither will Jacob’s God consider.”
WEB.8: Consider, you senseless among the people; you fools, when will you be wise?
WEB.9: He who implanted the ear, won’t he hear? He who formed the eye, won’t he see?
WEB.10: He who disciplines the nations, won’t he punish? He who teaches man knows.
WEB.11: Yahweh knows the thoughts of man, that they are futile.
WEB.12: Blessed is the man whom you discipline, Yah, and teach out of your law;
WEB.13: that you may give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit is dug for the wicked.
WEB.14: For Yahweh won’t reject his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
WEB.15: For judgment will return to righteousness. All the upright in heart shall follow it.
WEB.16: Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?
WEB.17: Unless Yahweh had been my help, my soul would have soon lived in silence.
WEB.18: When I said, “My foot is slipping!” Your loving kindness, Yahweh, held me up.
WEB.19: In the multitude of my thoughts within me, your comforts delight my soul.
WEB.20: Shall the throne of wickedness have fellowship with you, which brings about mischief by statute?
WEB.21: They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.
WEB.22: But Yahweh has been my high tower, my God, the rock of my refuge.
WEB.23: He has brought on them their own iniquity, and will cut them off in their own wickedness. Yahweh, our God, will cut them off.

CSB17.1:
LORD, God of vengeance —
God of vengeance, shine! ⓙ
CSB17.2:
Rise up, Judge of the earth;
repay the proud what they deserve. ⓚ
CSB17.3:
LORD, how long will the wicked —
how long will the wicked celebrate? ⓛ
CSB17.4:
They pour out arrogant words;
all the evildoers boast. ⓜ
CSB17.5:
LORD, they crush your people;
they oppress your heritage. ⓝ
CSB17.6:
They kill the widow and the resident alien
and murder the fatherless. ⓐ
CSB17.7:
They say, “The LORD doesn’t see it.
The God of Jacob doesn’t pay attention.” ⓑ
CSB17.8:
Pay attention, you stupid people!
Fools, when will you be wise? ⓒ
CSB17.9:
Can the one who shaped the ear not hear,
the one who formed the eye not see? ⓓ
CSB17.10:
The one who instructs nations,
the one who teaches mankind knowledge —
does he not discipline? ⓔ
CSB17.11:
The LORD knows the thoughts of mankind;
they are futile. ⓕ
CSB17.12:
LORD, how happy is anyone you discipline
and teach from your law ⓖ
CSB17.13:
to give him relief from troubled times
until a pit is dug for the wicked. ⓗ
CSB17.14:
The LORD will not leave his people
or abandon his heritage, ⓘ
CSB17.15:
for the administration of justice will again be righteous,
and all the upright in heart will follow [1] it. ⓙ
CSB17.16:
Who stands up for me against the wicked?
Who takes a stand for me against evildoers? ⓚ
CSB17.17:
If the LORD had not been my helper,
I would soon rest in the silence of death. ⓛ
CSB17.18:
If I say, “My foot is slipping,”
your faithful love will support me, LORD. ⓜ
CSB17.19:
When I am filled with cares,
your comfort brings me joy. ⓝ
CSB17.20:
Can a corrupt throne be your ally,
a throne that makes evil laws? ⓐ
CSB17.21:
They band together against the life of the righteous
and condemn the innocent to death. ⓑ
CSB17.22:
But the LORD is my refuge;
my God is the rock of my protection. ⓒ
CSB17.23:
He will pay them back for their sins
and destroy them for their evil.
The LORD our God will destroy them. ⓓ

AMP.1:
O LORD God, You to whom vengeance belongs,
O God, You to whom vengeance belongs, shine forth [in judgment]!
AMP.2:
Rise up, O Judge of the earth;
Give to the proud a fitting compensation.
AMP.3:
O LORD, how long will the wicked,
How long will the wicked rejoice in triumph?
AMP.4:
They pour out words, speaking arrogant things;
All who do evil boast proudly.
AMP.5:
They crush Your people, O LORD,
And afflict and abuse Your heritage.
AMP.6:
They kill the widow and the alien
And murder the fatherless.
AMP.7:
Yet they say, "The LORD does not see,
Nor does the God of Jacob (Israel) notice it."
AMP.8:
Consider thoughtfully, you senseless (stupid ones) among the people;
And you [dull-minded] fools, when will you become wise and understand?
AMP.9:
He who made the ear, does He not hear?
He who formed the eye, does He not see?
AMP.10:
He who instructs the nations,
Does He not rebuke and punish,
He who teaches man knowledge?
AMP.11:
The LORD knows the thoughts of man,
That they are a mere breath (vain, empty, futile).
AMP.12:
Blessed [with wisdom and prosperity] is the man whom You discipline and instruct, O LORD,
And whom You teach from Your law,
AMP.13:
That You may grant him [power to calm himself and find] peace in the days of adversity,
Until the pit is dug for the wicked and ungodly.
AMP.14:
For the LORD will not abandon His people,
Nor will He abandon His inheritance.
AMP.15:
For judgment will again be righteous,
And all the upright in heart will follow it.
AMP.16:
Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?
Who will take a stand for me against those who do wickedness?
AMP.17:
If the LORD had not been my help,
I would soon have dwelt in [the land of] silence.
AMP.18:
If I say, "My foot has slipped,"
Your compassion and lovingkindness, O LORD, will hold me up.
AMP.19:
When my anxious thoughts multiply within me,
Your comforts delight me.
AMP.20:
Can a throne of destruction be allied with You,
One which frames and devises mischief by decree [under the sacred name of law]?
AMP.21:
They band themselves together against the life of the righteous
And condemn the innocent to death.
AMP.22:
But the LORD has become my high tower and defense,
And my God the rock of my refuge.
AMP.23:
He has turned back their own wickedness upon them
And will destroy them by means of their own evil;
The LORD our God will wipe them out.

MSG.1:
God, put an end to evil;
avenging God, show your colors!
MSG.2:
Judge of the earth, take your stand;
throw the book at the arrogant.
MSG.3:
God, the wicked get away with murder—
how long will you let this go on?
MSG.4:
They brag and boast
and crow about their crimes!
MSG.5:
They walk all over your people, God,
exploit and abuse your precious people.
MSG.6:
They take out anyone who gets in their way;
if they can't use them, they kill them.
MSG.7:
They think, "God isn't looking,
Jacob's God is out to lunch."
MSG.8:
Well, think again, you idiots,
fools—how long before you get smart?
MSG.9:
Do you think Ear-Maker doesn't hear,
Eye-Shaper doesn't see?
MSG.10:
Do you think the trainer of nations doesn't correct,
the teacher of Adam doesn't know?
MSG.11:
God knows, all right—
knows your stupidity,
sees your shallowness.
MSG.12:
How blessed the man you train, God,
the woman you instruct in your Word,
MSG.13:
Providing a circle of quiet within the clamor of evil,
while a jail is being built for the wicked.
MSG.14:
God will never walk away from his people,
never desert his precious people.
MSG.15:
Rest assured that justice is on its way
and every good heart put right.
MSG.16:
Who stood up for me against the wicked?
Who took my side against evil workers?
MSG.17:
If God hadn't been there for me,
I never would have made it.
MSG.18:
The minute I said, "I'm slipping, I'm falling,"
your love, God, took hold and held me fast.
MSG.19:
When I was upset and beside myself,
you calmed me down and cheered me up.
MSG.20:
Can Misrule have anything in common with you?
Can Troublemaker pretend to be on your side?
MSG.21:
They ganged up on good people,
plotted behind the backs of the innocent.
MSG.22:
But God became my hideout,
God was my high mountain retreat,
MSG.23:
Then boomeranged their evil back on them:
for their evil ways he wiped them out,
our God cleaned them out for good.

NIV.1:
Psalm 94
O Lord, the God who avenges,
O God who avenges, shine forth.
NIV.2:
Rise up, O Judge of the earth;
pay back to the proud what they deserve.
NIV.3:
How long will the wicked, O Lord,
how long will the wicked be jubilant?
NIV.4:
They pour out arrogant words;
all the evildoers are full of boasting.
NIV.5:
They crush your people, O Lord;
they oppress your inheritance.
NIV.6:
They slay the widow and the alien;
they murder the fatherless.
NIV.7:
They say, “The Lord does not see;
the God of Jacob pays no heed.”
NIV.8:
Take heed, you senseless ones among the people;
you fools, when will you become wise?
NIV.9:
Does he who implanted the ear not hear?
Does he who formed the eye not see?
NIV.10:
Does he who disciplines nations not punish?
Does he who teaches man lack knowledge?
NIV.11:
The Lord knows the thoughts of man;
he knows that they are futile.
NIV.12:
Blessed is the man you discipline, O Lord,
the man you teach from your law;
NIV.13:
you grant him relief from days of trouble,
till a pit is dug for the wicked.
NIV.14:
For the Lord will not reject his people;
he will never forsake his inheritance.
NIV.15:
Judgment will again be founded on righteousness,
and all the upright in heart will follow it.
NIV.16:
Who will rise up for me against the wicked?
Who will take a stand for me against evildoers?
NIV.17:
Unless the Lord had given me help,
I would soon have dwelt in the silence of death.
NIV.18:
When I said, “My foot is slipping,”
your love, O Lord, supported me.
NIV.19:
When anxiety was great within me,
your consolation brought joy to my soul.
NIV.20:
Can a corrupt throne be allied with you—
one that brings on misery by its decrees?
NIV.21:
They band together against the righteous
and condemn the innocent to death.
NIV.22:
But the Lord has become my fortress,
and my God the rock in whom I take refuge.
NIV.23:
He will repay them for their sins
and destroy them for their wickedness;
the Lord our God will destroy them.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, you God of vengeance,
you God of vengeance, shine forth!
NRSVCE.2:
Rise up, O judge of the earth;
give to the proud what they deserve!
NRSVCE.3:
O LORD, how long shall the wicked,
how long shall the wicked exult?
NRSVCE.4:
They pour out their arrogant words;
all the evildoers boast.
NRSVCE.5:
They crush your people, O LORD,
and afflict your heritage.
NRSVCE.6:
They kill the widow and the stranger,
they murder the orphan,
NRSVCE.7:
and they say, “The LORD does not see;
the God of Jacob does not perceive.”
NRSVCE.8:
Understand, O dullest of the people;
fools, when will you be wise?
NRSVCE.9:
He who planted the ear, does he not hear?
He who formed the eye, does he not see?
NRSVCE.10:
He who disciplines the nations,
he who teaches knowledge to humankind,
does he not chastise?
NRSVCE.11:
The LORD knows our thoughts,
that they are but an empty breath.
NRSVCE.12:
Happy are those whom you discipline, O LORD,
and whom you teach out of your law,
NRSVCE.13:
giving them respite from days of trouble,
until a pit is dug for the wicked.
NRSVCE.14:
For the LORD will not forsake his people;
he will not abandon his heritage;
NRSVCE.15:
for justice will return to the righteous,
and all the upright in heart will follow it.
NRSVCE.16:
Who rises up for me against the wicked?
Who stands up for me against evildoers?
NRSVCE.17:
If the LORD had not been my help,
my soul would soon have lived in the land of silence.
NRSVCE.18:
When I thought, “My foot is slipping,”
your steadfast love, O LORD, held me up.
NRSVCE.19:
When the cares of my heart are many,
your consolations cheer my soul.
NRSVCE.20:
Can wicked rulers be allied with you,
those who contrive mischief by statute?
NRSVCE.21:
They band together against the life of the righteous,
and condemn the innocent to death.
NRSVCE.22:
But the LORD has become my stronghold,
and my God the rock of my refuge.
NRSVCE.23:
He will repay them for their iniquity
and wipe them out for their wickedness;
the LORD our God will wipe them out.

NET.1: O LORD, the God who avenges! O God who avenges, reveal your splendor!
NET.2: Rise up, O judge of the earth! Pay back the proud!
NET.3: O LORD, how long will the wicked, how long will the wicked celebrate?
NET.4: They spew out threats and speak defiantly; all the evildoers boast.
NET.5: O LORD, they crush your people; they oppress the nation that belongs to you.
NET.6: They kill the widow and the one residing outside his native land, and they murder the fatherless.
NET.7: Then they say, “The LORD does not see this; the God of Jacob does not take notice of it.”
NET.8: Take notice of this, you ignorant people! You fools, when will you ever understand?
NET.9: Does the one who makes the human ear not hear? Does the one who forms the human eye not see?
NET.10: Does the one who disciplines the nations not punish? He is the one who imparts knowledge to human beings!
NET.11: The LORD knows that peoples’ thoughts are morally bankrupt.
NET.12: How blessed is the one whom you instruct, O LORD, the one whom you teach from your law,
NET.13: in order to protect him from times of trouble, until the wicked are destroyed.
NET.14: Certainly the LORD does not forsake his people; he does not abandon the nation that belongs to him.
NET.15: For justice will prevail, and all the morally upright will be vindicated.
NET.16: Who will rise up to defend me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?
NET.17: If the LORD had not helped me, I would have laid down in the silence of death.
NET.18: If I say, “My foot is slipping,” your loyal love, O LORD, supports me.
NET.19: When worries threaten to overwhelm me, your soothing touch makes me happy.
NET.20: Cruel rulers are not your allies, those who make oppressive laws.
NET.21: They conspire against the blameless, and condemn to death the innocent.
NET.22: But the LORD will protect me, and my God will shelter me.
NET.23: He will pay them back for their sin. He will destroy them because of their evil; the LORD our God will destroy them.
